name: rls_disabled_in_public
title: RLS Disabled in Public
description: Detects cases where row level security (RLS) has not been enabled on tables in schemas exposed to PostgREST
detail: Table \`public.ai_usage_ledger_2026_04\` is public, but RLS has not been enabled.

name: rls_disabled_in_public
title: RLS Disabled in Public
description: Detects cases where row level security (RLS) has not been enabled on tables in schemas exposed to PostgREST
detail: Table \`public.ai_usage_ledger_2026_05\` is public, but RLS has not been enabled.

name: extension_in_public
title: Extension in Public
description: Detects extensions installed in the \`public\` schema.
detail: Extension \`vector\` is installed in the public schema. Move it to another schema.

name: public_bucket_allows_listing
title: Public Bucket Allows Listing
description: Detects public storage buckets with a broad SELECT policy on `storage.objects`, which allows clients to list all files in the bucket.
detail: Public bucket `backgrounds` has 1 broad SELECT policy on `storage.objects` (Anyone can view backgrounds), allowing clients to list all files. Public buckets don't need this for object URL access and it may expose more data than intended.

name: public_bucket_allows_listing
title: Public Bucket Allows Listing
description: Detects public storage buckets with a broad SELECT policy on `storage.objects`, which allows clients to list all files in the bucket.
detail: Public bucket `card-backs` has 1 broad SELECT policy on `storage.objects` (Public read access to card backs), allowing clients to list all files. Public buckets don't need this for object URL access and it may expose more data than intended.

name: public_bucket_allows_listing
title: Public Bucket Allows Listing
description: Detects public storage buckets with a broad SELECT policy on `storage.objects`, which allows clients to list all files in the bucket.
detail: Public bucket `custom-icons` has 1 broad SELECT policy on `storage.objects` (Custom icons are publicly accessible), allowing clients to list all files. Public buckets don't need this for object URL access and it may expose more data than intended.

name: public_bucket_allows_listing
title: Public Bucket Allows Listing
description: Detects public storage buckets with a broad SELECT policy on `storage.objects`, which allows clients to list all files in the bucket.
detail: Public bucket `tarot-images` has 1 broad SELECT policy on `storage.objects` (Public read access for tarot images), allowing clients to list all files. Public buckets don't need this for object URL access and it may expose more data than intended.

name: auth_leaked_password_protection
title: Leaked Password Protection Disabled
description: Leaked password protection is currently disabled.
detail: Supabase Auth prevents the use of compromised passwords by checking against HaveIBeenPwned.org. Enable this feature to enhance security.

name: auth_insufficient_mfa_options
title: Insufficient MFA Options
description: This project has too few multi-factor authentication (MFA) options enabled.
detail: Your project has too few MFA options enabled, which may weaken account security. Enable more MFA methods to enhance security.

name: auth_rls_initplan
title: Auth RLS Initialization Plan
description: Detects if calls to \`current_setting()\` and \`auth.<function>()\` in RLS policies are being unnecessarily re-evaluated for each row
detail: Table \`public.compat_responses\` has a row level security policy \`compat_responses_select_participant\` that re-evaluates current_setting() or auth.<function>() for each row. This produces suboptimal query performance at scale. Resolve the issue by replacing \`auth.<function>()\` with \`(select auth.<function>())\`. See [docs](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security#call-functions-with-select) for more info.

name: multiple_permissive_policies
title: Multiple Permissive Policies
description: Detects if multiple permissive row level security policies are present on a table for the same \`role\` and \`action\` (e.g. insert). Multiple permissive policies are suboptimal for performance as each policy must be executed for every relevant query.
detail: Table \`public.advisor_verifications\` has multiple permissive policies for role \`anon\` for action \`UPDATE\`. Policies include \`{advisor_verifications_admin_update,advisor_verifications_update_own_pending}\`
